title: LimozaVulnerability
description: One record per vulnerability. lve_id is a vendor-neutral sequential identifier. CVE data is a sub-object — present only when a CVE exists. Array fields carry a source discriminator. Cross-references between array items use JSON-LD @id / {"@id": "..."} associations.

advisory_ref.description: JSON-LD reference to an advisory in advisories[] by its @id.

upstream_ref.description: JSON-LD reference to an upstream project in upstream[] by its @id.

cwe.title: CweDictionaryEntry
cwe.description: Standalone CWE weakness definition — one row per weakness, shared across all LVEs. This is NOT part of a vulnerability record; it lives independently in the `cwe` dictionary table. A vulnerability record's cwes[].id joins here by cwe_id. Populated on-reference from the CWE-CAPEC json_repo/W dictionary (mitigations, consequences, likelihood, …) so consumers have full weakness context from the database alone.

cwe_id.description: Primary key. Matches a vulnerability record's cwes[].id.

examples: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

common_consequences.description: Impact of the weakness when exploited.

modes_of_introduction.description: Where/when the weakness is typically introduced.

detection_methods.description: How the weakness can be detected.

related_weaknesses.description: Parent/child relationships within the CWE tree.

synced_at.description: When this dictionary entry was last refreshed.

lve_id.description: Stable vendor-neutral sequential identifier. Auto-generated by the DB. Never derived from CVE or any vendor ID.

aliases.description: All known alternative identifiers for this vulnerability — CVE IDs, vendor advisory IDs (RHSA-2023:1234, USN-6009-1, ADV240001), GHSA IDs, etc. Searchable tags to find the LVE by any identifier. Always uppercase. Accumulated across all sources, no source discriminator needed.

has_exploit.description: True when exploits[] is non-empty. Derived field for fast filtering.

cve.description: CVE data from MITRE/NVD. Null for LVEs without a CVE (vendor-only advisories like Microsoft ADV, BSI WID). Only NVD writes this object.

epss.description: FIRST EPSS score. CVE-centric, only meaningful when a CVE exists.

kev.description: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerability entry. CVE-centric.

ssvc.description: CISA SSVC triage decision points. CVE-centric.

titles.description: Vulnerability titles from all sources. Unique per (source, advisory). advisory is the RHSA-ID for advisory-level titles, a source ref like 'csaf_vex:CVE-...' for CVE-level titles, or null (e.g. NVD).

descriptions.description: Vulnerability descriptions from all sources. Unique per (source, advisory). advisory is the RHSA-ID for advisory-level descriptions, a source ref like 'csaf_vex:CVE-...' for CVE-level descriptions, or null (e.g. NVD).

cvss.description: CVSS scores from all sources. One entry per (vector, source). severity is the human-readable label — may differ from what the score mechanically implies. advisory links to the advisory that provided this score — null for sources without a formal advisory.

product_id.description: Vendor-specific product identifier. Microsoft only: CVRF ProductID this score applies to.

cwes.description: CWE weakness identifiers. One entry per (id, source) — sources may disagree. advisory links to the advisory that provided this classification — null for sources without a formal advisory. This is the per-record classification only; the shared weakness definition (mitigations, consequences, likelihood, …) lives once in the cwe dictionary table, keyed by id.

id.description: CWE id; joins to the cwe dictionary table for the full weakness definition.

references.description: Links to patches, advisories, and other resources. One entry per url. advisory links to the advisory that provided this reference — null for sources without a formal advisory.

advisories.description: Vendor advisories covering this vulnerability. Each advisory is identified by @id for cross-referencing from titles[], descriptions[], cvss[], cwes[], and packages[].

@id.description: Unique advisory identifier within this LVE. Referenced by advisory: {"@id": "..."} in other arrays.

packages.description: PURLs covered by this advisory within this LVE.

vendor_data.description: Vendor-specific fields with no universal equivalent (e.g. Microsoft AggregatedSeverity, Ubuntu Priority, RedHat Impact).

upstream.description: Upstream project fix information. One entry per upstream project affected by this CVE. Distro packages in packages[] reference their upstream via upstream_ref. Multiple entries when the CVE affects several upstream projects (e.g. curl and openssl).

@id.description: Unique identifier for this upstream entry within this LVE. Referenced by packages[].upstream.

fix_version.description: Latest or recommended fix version. Convenience field — use ranges[] for full accuracy.

fix_commit.description: Upstream fix commit hash or tag.

versions.description: Explicit list of all affected version strings from OSV/GHSA. Null when not provided by the source.

ranges.description: Affected version ranges from the upstream source (e.g. GHSA/OSV). Each entry defines one contiguous affected range. A vulnerability may have multiple ranges across different release branches.

introduced.description: First affected version. '0' means all versions from the beginning.

fixed.description: First version that is no longer affected (exclusive upper bound).

last_affected.description: Last affected version (inclusive upper bound). Used when no fix exists yet.

packages.description: Affected and fixed package entries across all distros and ecosystems. One entry per (purl, source). purl identifies the package without version — version info lives in ranges[]. advisory links to the advisory that reported this fix. upstream links to the upstream project in upstream[].

purl.description: Package identity without version, e.g. pkg:rpm/redhat/kernel?distro=el9.

affected_state.description: Whether this package/release is affected. affected=vulnerable; not_affected=package exists but CVE does not apply (e.g. code compiled out); not_applicable=package does not exist in this release at all; unknown=vendor has not stated. Note: a package can be affected_state=affected and remediation_state=fixed simultaneously — fixed lives on the remediation axis, not here.

remediation_state.description: Remediation availability. fixed=patch released; pending=in progress; will_not_fix=vendor will not fix (EOL, out-of-scope, etc.); none=no fix available yet; unknown=not stated.

status_raw.description: Original vendor status string before normalisation, e.g. 'known_not_affected', 'none_available'.

vex_justification.description: CISA/OASIS VEX justification when affected_state=not_affected. Sourced primarily from CSAF flags[].label (Red Hat, SUSE, and other CSAF publishers). Occasionally from OpenVEX justification field when vendors publish in that format.

ranges.description: OSV-style version ranges. Each entry has a type (ECOSYSTEM, GIT, SEMVER) and events[] listing introduced/fixed/last_affected bounds. For distro packages one range with introduced=0 and fixed=<NVR>. Null for not_affected / unknown.

events.description: Ordered list of version events. Each event is a single-key object: {introduced}, {fixed}, or {last_affected}.

mitigations.description: Workarounds and mitigation steps that reduce risk without applying a patch. One entry per (source, advisory). purls optionally scopes the mitigation to specific packages — null means it applies to all affected packages.

purls.description: PURLs this mitigation applies to. Null means all packages in this LVE.

impacts.description: Descriptions of what an attacker can concretely achieve — beyond the CVSS vector. One entry per (source, advisory). Sourced from CSAF threats[category=impact] and vendor statement notes.

value.description: Free-text impact description, e.g. vendor severity rating with justification.

exploits.description: Exploit intelligence. One entry per exploit artifact.

history.description: Append-only log of all changes to this LVE.
